{\n   154→    if (listing?.is_full && listing?.isMember) {\n   155→      fetchRatings();\n   156→    }\n   157→  }, [listing?.is_full, listing?.isMember, fetchRatings]);\n   158→\n   159→  const handleRatingSubmit = async (memberId: number) => {\n   160→    const score = ratingScores[memberId];\n   161→    if (!score) return;\n   162→    setRatingSaving(memberId);\n   163→    setRatingMessage(\"\");\n   164→    try {\n   165→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/ratings\", {\n   166→        method: \"POST\",\n   167→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   168→        body: JSON.stringify({\n   169→          listingId: parseInt(id as string, 10),\n   170→          ratedUserId: memberId,\n   171→          score,\n   172→          comment: ratingComments[memberId] || \"\",\n   173→        }),\n   174→      });\n   175→      const data = await res.json();\n   176→      if (!res.ok) {\n   177→        setRatingMessage(data.error || \"Failed to submit rating\");\n   178→      } else {\n   179→        setRatingMessage(\"Rating saved!\");\n   180→        setGivenRatings((prev) => ({\n   181→          ...prev,\n   182→          [memberId]: { score, comment: ratingComments[memberId] || \"\" },\n   183→        }));\n   184→      }\n   185→    } catch {\n   186→      setRatingMessage(\"Failed to submit rating\");\n   187→    } finally {\n   188→      setRatingSaving(null);\n   189→    }\n   190→  };\n   191→\n   192→  const handleJoin = async () => {\n   193→    setJoining(true);\n   194→    setError(\"\");\n   195→    try {\n   196→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/join`, { method: \"POST\" });\n   197→      const data = await res.json();\n   198→      if (!res.ok) {\n   199→        if (res.status === 401) {\n   200→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n   201→          return;\n   202→        }\n   203→        setError(data.error);\n   204→        return;\n   205→      }\n   206→      await fetchListing();\n   207→    } catch {\n   208→      setError(\"Failed to join. Please try again.\");\n   209→    } finally {\n   210→      setJoining(false);\n   211→    }\n   212→  };\n   213→\n   214→  const handleApply = async () => {\n   215→    setApplying(true);\n   216→    setError(\"\");\n   217→    try {\n   218→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/apply`, { method: \"POST\" });\n   219→      const data = await res.json();\n   220→      if (!res.ok) {\n   221→        if (res.status === 401) {\n   222→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n   223→          return;\n   224→        }\n   225→        setError(data.error);\n   226→        return;\n   227→      }\n   228→      await fetchListing();\n   229→    } catch {\n   230→      setError(\"Failed to apply. Please try again.\");\n   231→    } finally {\n   232→      setApplying(false);\n   233→    }\n   234→  };\n   235→\n   236→  const handleDecision = async (applicationId: number, action: \"approve\" | \"reject\") => {\n   237→    setDecidingId(applicationId);\n   238→    setError(\"\");\n   239→    try {\n   240→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/applications`, {\n   241→        method: \"PATCH\",\n   242→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   243→        body: JSON.stringify({ applicationId, action }),\n   244→      });\n   245→      const data = await res.json();\n   246→      if (!res.ok) {\n   247→        setError(data.error);\n   248→        return;\n   249→      }"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"2735c7dc-fe9a-4a38-8574-2e0c9349d579","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:03:58.793Z"}

Please try again.\");\n   302→    } finally {\n   303→      setAutoTelegramLoading(false);\n   304→    }\n   305→  };\n   306→\n   307→  const isValidDiscordLink = (link: string) => {\n   308→    return /^https:\\/\\/(discord\\.gg|discord\\.com\\/invite)\\/[\\w-]+$/.test(link.trim());\n   309→  };\n   310→\n   311→  const discordLinkError = discordLink && !isValidDiscordLink(discordLink)\n   312→    ? \"Link must start with https://discord.gg/ or https://discord.com/invite/\"\n   313→    : \"\";\n   314→\n   315→  const handleDiscordSave = async () => {\n   316→    if (!isValidDiscordLink(discordLink)) return;\n   317→    setDiscordSaving(true);\n   318→    setError(\"\");\n   319→    try {\n   320→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/discord`, {\n   321→        method: \"POST\",\n   322→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   323→        body: JSON.stringify({ discordLink: discordLink.trim() }),\n   324→      });\n   325→      const data = await res.json();\n   326→      if (!res.ok) {\n   327→        setError(data.error);\n   328→        return;\n   329→      }\n   330→      await fetchListing();\n   331→    } catch {\n   332→      setError(\"Failed to save link. Please try again.\");\n   333→    } finally {\n   334→      setDiscordSaving(false);\n   335→    }\n   336→  };\n   337→\n   338→  const handleAutoDiscord = async () => {\n   339→    setAutoDiscordLoading(true);\n   340→    setError(\"\");\n   341→    try {\n   342→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/auto-discord`);\n   343→      const data = await res.json();\n   344→      if (!res.ok) {\n   345→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to generate Discord link\");\n   346→        return;\n   347→      }\n   348→      setAutoDiscordLink(data.inviteUrl);\n   349→    } catch {\n   350→      setError(\"Failed to connect. Please try again.\");\n   351→    } finally {\n   352→      setAutoDiscordLoading(false);\n   353→    }\n   354→  };\n   355→\n   356→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   357→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   358→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   359→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   360→      mixed: \"Mixed (voice + text)\",\n   361→    };\n   362→    return labels[f] || f;\n   363→  };\n   364→\n   365→  if (loading) {\n   366→    return (\n   367→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n   368→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n   369→      </div>\n   370→    );\n   371→  }\n   372→\n   373→  if (fetchError) {\n   374→    return (\n   375→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n   376→        <h2 className=\"text-2xl mb-2\">Something went wrong</h2>\n   377→        <p style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>\n   378→          Failed to load this listing. Please try again.\n   379→        </p>\n   380→        <button\n   381→          onClick={() => { setFetchError(false); setLoading(true); fetchListing(); }}\n   382→          className=\"mt-4 px-4 py-2 rounded cursor-pointer\"\n   383→          style={{ background: \"var(--color-accent)\", color: \"white\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   384→        >\n   385→          Retry\n   386→        </button>\n   387→      </div>\n   388→    );\n   389→  }\n   390→\n   391→  if (notAvailable) {\n   392→    return (\n   393→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n   394→        <h2 className=\"text-2xl mb-2\">This listing is no longer available</h2>\n   395→        <p style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>\n   396→          This reading group has reached its maximum size and is no longer visible to non-members.\n   397→        </p>\n   398→        <button\n   399→          onClick={() => router.push(\"/\")}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"91c90e0c-fa3a-43cf-b21f-5ef252e2a0dc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:02.539Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LqddBvbVEbTtgj8Kgaw1aQ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Notification {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  listing_id: number;\n     9→  type: string;\n    10→  message: string;\n    11→  is_read: number;\n    12→  created_at: string;\n    13→  book_title: string;\n    14→}\n    15→\n    16→export default function NotificationsPage() {\n    17→  const [notifications, setNotifications] = useState<Notification[]>([]);\n    18→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    19→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    20→\n    21→  useEffect(() => {\n    22→    fetch(\"/api/notifications\")\n    23→      .then((r) => {\n    24→        if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load notifications\");\n    25→        return r.json();\n    26→      })\n    27→      .then((data) => {\n    28→        setNotifications(data.notifications || []);\n    29→        // Mark all as read (fire-and-forget with silent catch)\n    30→        fetch(\"/api/notifications\", {\n    31→          method: \"PATCH\",\n    32→          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n    34→        }).catch(() => {});\n    35→      })\n    36→      .catch(() => {\n    37→        setError(\"Could not load notifications. Please try again.\");\n    38→      })\n    39→      .finally(() => {\n    40→        setLoading(false);\n    41→      });\n    42→  }, []);\n    43→\n    44→  if (loading) {\n    45→    return (\n    46→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n    47→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n    48→      </div>\n    49→    );\n    50→  }\n    51→\n    52→  if (error) {\n    53→    return (\n    54→      <div className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n    55→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-4 sm:mb-6\">Notifications</h1>\n    56→        <div className=\"text-center py-12\">\n    57→          <p\n    58→            className=\"text-sm mb-4\"\n    59→            style={{ color: \"var(--color-error)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    60→            role=\"alert\"\n    61→          >\n    62→            {error}\n    63→          </p>\n    64→          <button\n    65→            onClick={() => window.location.reload()}\n    66→            className=\"btn-primary\"\n    67→          >\n    68→            Try again\n    69→          </button>\n    70→        </div>\n    71→      </div>\n    72→    );\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  return (\n    76→    <div className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n    77→      <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-4 sm:mb-6\">Notifications</h1>\n    78→\n    79→      {notifications.length === 0 ? (\n    80→        <div className=\"text-center py-12\">\n    81→          <p style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>\n    82→            No notifications yet\n    83→          </p>\n    84→        </div>\n    85→      ) : (\n    86→        <div className=\"space-y-2\">\n    87→          {notifications.map((n) => (\n    88→            <Link\n    89→              key={n.id}\n    90→              href={n.listing_id ? `/listings/${n.listing_id}` : \"#\"}\n    91→              className=\"card block hover:shadow-sm transition-shadow\"\n    92→              style={{\n    93→                borderLeftWidth: \"3px\",\n    94→                borderLeftColor: n.is_read\n    95→                  ? \"var(--color-border)\"\n    96→                  : \"var(--color-accent)\",\n    97→              }}\n    98→              aria-label={`${!n.is_read ? \"Unread: \" : \"\"}${n.message}`}\n    99→            >\n   100→              {!n.is_read && (\n   101→                <span className=\"sr-only\">Unread</span>\n   102→              )}\n   103→              <p\n   104→                className=\"text-sm\"\n   105→                style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   106→              >\n   107→                {n.message}\n   108→              </p>\n   109→              <p\n   110→                className=\"text-xs mt-1\"\n   111→                style={{\n   112→                  color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   113→                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   114→                }}\n   115→              >\n   116→                {new Date(n.created_at).toLocaleDateString(\"en-US\", {\n   117→                  month: \"short\",\n   118→                  day: \"numeric\",\n   119→                  hour: \"numeric\",\n   120→                  minute: \"2-digit\",\n   121→                })}\n   122→              </p>\n   123→            </Link>\n   124→          ))}\n   125→        </div>\n   126→      )}\n   127→    </div>\n   128→  );\n   129→}\n   130→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"fdc1d074-4041-4c7b-8fdf-4fdb6a8c319a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:02.589Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PN7LC8YgmPjKvrda7bsisz","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import BookSearch from \"@/components/BookSearch\";\n     6→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     7→\n     8→export default function CreateListingPage() {\n     9→  const router = useRouter();\n    10→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    11→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    12→\n    13→  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = useState(\"\");\n    14→  const [bookAuthor, setBookAuthor] = useState(\"\");\n    15→  const [bookCoverUrl, setBookCoverUrl] = useState(\"\");\n    16→  const [bookOlid, setBookOlid] = useState(\"\");\n    17→  const [language, setLanguage] = useState(\"English\");\n    18→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    19→  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState(\"\");\n    20→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"text\");\n    21→  const [maxGroupSize, setMaxGroupSize] = useState(\"4\");\n    22→  const [requiresApproval, setRequiresApproval] = useState(false);\n    23→  const [platformPreference, setPlatformPreference] = useState(\"telegram\");\n    24→\n    25→  const handleBookSelect = (book: {\n    26→    title: string;\n    27→    author: string;\n    28→    coverUrl: string;\n    29→    olid: string;\n    30→  }) => {\n    31→    setBookTitle(book.title);\n    32→    setBookAuthor(book.author);\n    33→    setBookCoverUrl(book.coverUrl);\n    34→    setBookOlid(book.olid);\n    35→  };\n    36→\n    37→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    38→    e.preventDefault();\n    39→    setError(\"\");\n    40→    setLoading(true);\n    41→\n    42→    try {\n    43→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/listings/create\", {\n    44→        method: \"POST\",\n    45→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    46→        body: JSON.stringify({\n    47→          bookTitle,\n    48→          bookAuthor,\n    49→          bookCoverUrl,\n    50→          bookOlid,\n    51→          language,\n    52→          readingPace,\n    53→          startDate,\n    54→          meetingFormat,\n    55→          maxGroupSize,\n    56→          requiresApproval,\n    57→          platformPreference,\n    58→        }),\n    59→      });\n    60→\n    61→      const data = await res.json();\n    62→\n    63→      if (!res.ok) {\n    64→        if (res.status === 401) {\n    65→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    66→          return;\n    67→        }\n    68→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to create listing\");\n    69→        return;\n    70→      }\n    71→\n    72→      router.push(`/listings/${data.id}`);\n    73→    } catch {\n    74→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n    75→    } finally {\n    76→      setLoading(false);\n    77→    }\n    78→  };\n    79→\n    80→  return (\n    81→    <div className=\"max-w-lg mx-auto\">\n    82→      <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-2\">Start a reading group</h1>\n    83→      <p\n    84→        className=\"mb-6 sm:mb-8\"\n    85→        style={{\n    86→          color: {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XSZAPvz6VULQdGeJH8hEQn","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Simple in-memory rate limiter for auth endpoints.\n     3→ * Uses a sliding window approach keyed by identifier (IP + route).\n     4→ * Not shared across cluster workers — acceptable for SQLite single-process setup.\n     5→ */\n     6→\n     7→interface RateLimitEntry {\n     8→  timestamps: number[];\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const store = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>();\n    12→\n    13→// Clean up expired entries every 5 minutes\n    14→// Guard against multiple intervals on Next.js hot reload\n    15→const CLEANUP_INTERVAL = 5 * 60 * 1000;\n    16→const globalObj = globalThis as unknown as { __rateLimitCleanup?: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> };\n    17→if (!globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup) {\n    18→  globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup = setInterval(() => {\n    19→    const now = Date.now();\n    20→    for (const [key, entry] of store) {\n    21→      entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < 15 * 60 * 1000);\n    22→      if (entry.timestamps.length === 0) {\n    23→        store.delete(key);\n    24→      }\n    25→    }\n    26→  }, CLEANUP_INTERVAL);\n    27→}\n    28→\n    29→/**\n    30→ * Check if a request should be rate limited.\n    31→ * @param key - Unique identifier (e.g., IP + email for login, IP for register)\n    32→ * @param maxAttempts - Maximum attempts allowed in the window\n    33→ * @param windowMs - Time window in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes)\n    34→ * @returns Object with allowed boolean and retryAfter seconds (0 if allowed)\n    35→ */\n    36→export function checkRateLimit(\n    37→  key: string,\n    38→  maxAttempts: number = 5,\n    39→  windowMs: number = 15 * 60 * 1000\n    40→): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter: number } {\n    41→  const now = Date.now();\n    42→  let entry = store.get(key);\n    43→\n    44→  if (!entry) {\n    45→    entry = { timestamps: [] };\n    46→    store.set(key, entry);\n    47→  }\n    48→\n    49→  // Remove expired timestamps\n    50→  entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < windowMs);\n    51→\n    52→  if (entry.timestamps.length >= maxAttempts) {\n    53→    const oldestInWindow = entry.timestamps[0];\n    54→    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((oldestInWindow + windowMs - now) / 1000);\n    55→    return { allowed: false, retryAfter };\n    56→  }\n    57→\n    58→  entry.timestamps.push(now);\n    59→  return { allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 };\n    60→}\n    61→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"ebe88ce1-d34d-46ba-a8a8-f6fbcec7d349","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:13.562Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XtojXXRYYC2GhbtcUjBffE","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n     2→\n     3→const isConfigured =\n     4→  !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n     5→  !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n     6→  !!process.env.SMTP_FROM;\n     7→\n     8→let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n     9→\n    10→function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n    11→  if (!isConfigured) return null;\n    12→  if (!transporter) {\n    13→    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n    14→      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    15→      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n    16→      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n    17→      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n    18→        ? {\n    19→            auth: {\n    20→              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    21→              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    22→            },\n    23→          }\n    24→        : {}),\n    25→    });\n    26→  }\n    27→  return transporter;\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→export async function sendEmail(\n    31→  to: string,\n    32→  subject: string,\n    33→  text: string,\n    34→  html?: string\n    35→): Promise<boolean> {\n    36→  const t = getTransporter();\n    37→  if (!t) return false;\n    38→\n    39→  try {\n    40→    await t.sendMail({\n    41→      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n    42→      to,\n    43→      subject,\n    44→      text,\n    45→      html: html || text,\n    46→    });\n    47→    return true;\n    48→  } catch (err) {\n    49→    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    50→    return false;\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→export function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n    55→  return isConfigured;\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"45939c3d-a2e5-415d-9d01-e41ae53e383f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:13.696Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01VzCZTBfpEGhziRx3Vn9tpr","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n     6→ * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n     7→ * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n    11→ * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n    12→ * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n    13→ * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n    18→\n    19→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    20→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→/**\n    24→ * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n    25→ */\n    26→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    27→  method: string,\n    28→  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n    29→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n    30→  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    31→    method: \"POST\",\n    32→    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n    34→  });\n    35→  return res.json();\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Get bot info (username, id).\n    40→ */\n    41→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    42→  id: number;\n    43→  username: string;\n    44→  first_name: string;\n    45→} | null> {\n    46→  const res = await callApi<{\n    47→    id: number;\n    48→    username: string;\n    49→    first_name: string;\n    50→  }>(\"getMe\");\n    51→  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→/**\n    55→ * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n    56→ * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n    57→ *\n    58→ * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n    59→ *\n    60→ * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n    61→ * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n    62→ */\n    63→export async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n    64→  listingId: number\n    65→): Promise<string | null> {\n    66→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    67→  if (!bot) return null;\n    68→  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n    69→}\n    70→\n    71→/**\n    72→ * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n    73→ */\n    74→export async function createChatInviteLink(\n    75→  chatId: number | string\n    76→): Promise<string | null> {\n    77→  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    78→    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    79→    {\n    80→      chat_id: chatId,\n    81→      name: \"Bookmate Reading Group\",\n    82→      creates_join_request: false,\n    83→    }\n    84→  );\n    85→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    86→    return res.result.invite_link;\n    87→  }\n    88→  return null;\n    89→}\n    90→\n    91→/**\n    92→ * Export the existing invite link for a chat (fallback).\n    93→ */\n    94→export async function exportChatInviteLink(\n    95→  chatId: number | string\n    96→): Promise<string | null> {\n    97→  const res = await callApi<string>(\"exportChatInviteLink\", {\n    98→    chat_id: chatId,\n    99→  });\n   100→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n   101→    return res.result;\n   102→  }\n   103→  return null;\n   104→}\n   105→\n   106→/**\n   107→ * Escape HTML special characters for Telegram HTML parse mode.\n   108→ * Telegram's HTML parser interprets <, >, &, and \" — all must be escaped\n   109→ * in user-supplied content to prevent message injection.\n   110→ */\n   111→export function escapeHtml(text: string): string {\n   112→  return text\n   113→    .replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\")\n   114→    .replace(/</g, \"&lt;\")\n   115→    .replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\")\n   116→    .replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→/**\n   120→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n   121→ */\n   122→export async function sendMessage(\n   123→  chatId: number | string,\n   124→  text: string\n   125→): Promise<boolean> {\n   126→  const res = await callApi(\"sendMessage\", {\n   127→    chat_id: chatId,\n   128→    text,\n   129→    parse_mode: \"HTML\",\n   130→  });\n   131→  return res.ok;\n   132→}\n   133→\n   134→/**\n   135→ * Set up webhook URL for receiving Telegram updates.\n   136→ */\n   137→export async function setWebhook(url: string): Promise<boolean> {\n   138→  const res = await callApi(\"setWebhook\", {\n   139→    url,\n   140→    allowed_updates: [\"my_chat_member\", \"message\"],\n   141→  });\n   142→  return res.ok;\n   143→}\n   144→\n   145→/**\n   146→ * Parse the listing ID from a /start command payload.\n   147→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   148→ */\n   149→export function parseListingIdFromPayload(\n   150→  payload: string\n   151→): number | null {\n   152→  const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   153→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   154→}\n   155→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"787da111-6bdb-4820-b93c-560476bc2842","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:16.969Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012YKhR4P2AzJEs131qyUreu","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await getSession();\n    11→  if (!session.userId) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json(\n    13→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    14→      { status: 401 }\n    15→    );\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Discord bot is not configured\" },\n    21→      { status: 503 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  const { id } = await params;\n    26→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    27→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  const listing = db\n    32→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    33→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    40→    return NextResponse.json(\n    41→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n    42→      { status: 403 }\n    43→    );\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n    49→      { status: 400 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n    61→\n    62→  return NextResponse.json({\n    63→    inviteUrl,\n    64→    instructions:\n    65→      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n    66→      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n    67→  });\n    68→}\n    69→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"d64940c7-71e1-463e-ac7c-da8c5ff2dacf","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:17.236Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01V54Gn9YRYTZ2MfL1jKRWBr","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     5→\n     6→interface BookResult {\n     7→  key: string;\n     8→  title: string;\n     9→  author_name?: string[];\n    10→  cover_i?: number;\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→interface Props {\n    14→  onSelect: (book: {\n    15→    title: string;\n    16→    author: string;\n    17→    coverUrl: string;\n    18→    olid: string;\n    19→  }) => void;\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→export default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n    23→  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    24→  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n    25→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    26→  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n    27→  const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(-1);\n    28→  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    29→  const abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n    30→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    31→  const listboxRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    32→\n    33→  useEffect(() => {\n    34→    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    35→      if (\n    36→        containerRef.current &&\n    37→        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n    38→      ) {\n    39→        setOpen(false);\n    40→      }\n    41→    };\n    42→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    43→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    44→  }, []);\n    45→\n    46→  useEffect(() => {\n    47→    if (query.length < 3) {\n    48→      setResults([]);\n    49→      return;\n    50→    }\n    51→\n    52→    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    53→    if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n    54→\n    55→    const controller = new AbortController();\n    56→    abortRef.current = controller;\n    57→\n    58→    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n    59→      setLoading(true);\n    60→      try {\n    61→        const res = await fetch(\n    62→          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`,\n    63→          { signal: controller.signal }\n    64→        );\n    65→        const data = await res.json();\n    66→        setResults(data.docs || []);\n    67→        setOpen(true);\n    68→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n    69→      } catch (err) {\n    70→        if ((err as Error).name !== \"AbortError\") {\n    71→          setResults([]);\n    72→        }\n    73→      } finally {\n    74→        if (!controller.signal.aborted) {\n    75→          setLoading(false);\n    76→        }\n    77→      }\n    78→    }, 400);\n    79→\n    80→    return () => {\n    81→      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    82→      controller.abort();\n    83→    };\n    84→  }, [query]);\n    85→\n    86→  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    87→    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    88→    onSelect({\n    89→      title: book.title,\n    90→      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n    91→      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n    92→        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n    93→        : \"\",\n    94→      olid,\n    95→    });\n    96→    setQuery(book.title);\n    97→    setOpen(false);\n    98→    setActiveIndex(-1);\n    99→  };\n   100→\n   101→  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {\n   102→    if (!open || results.length === 0) return;\n   103→\n   104→    switch (e.key) {\n   105→      case \"ArrowDown\":\n   106→        e.preventDefault();\n   107→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev < results.length - 1 ? prev + 1 : 0));\n   108→        break;\n   109→      case \"ArrowUp\":\n   110→        e.preventDefault();\n   111→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev > 0 ? prev - 1 : results.length - 1));\n   112→        break;\n   113→      case \"Enter\":\n   114→        e.preventDefault();\n   115→        if (activeIndex >= 0 && activeIndex < results.length) {\n   116→          handleSelect(results[activeIndex]);\n   117→        }\n   118→        break;\n   119→      case \"Escape\":\n   120→        setOpen(false);\n   121→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n   122→        break;\n   123→    }\n   124→  };\n   125→\n   126→  // Scroll active option into view\n   127→  useEffect(() => {\n   128→    if (activeIndex >= 0 && listboxRef.current) {\n   129→      const activeEl = listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex] as HTMLElement;\n   130→      if (activeEl) {\n   131→        activeEl.scrollIntoView({ block: \"nearest\" });\n   132→      }\n   133→    }\n   134→  }, [activeIndex]);\n   135→\n   136→  const activeDescendant =\n   137→    activeIndex >= 0 && results[activeIndex]\n   138→      ? `book-option-${results[activeIndex].key}`\n   139→      : undefined;\n   140→\n   141→  return (\n   142→    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n   143→      <input\n   144→        type=\"text\"\n   145→        className=\"input-field\"\n   146→        value={query}\n   147→        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n   148→        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}\n   149→        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n   150→        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n   151→        role=\"combobox\"\n   152→        aria-expanded={open && results.length > 0}\n   153→        aria-haspopup=\"listbox\"\n   154→        aria-controls=\"book-search-listbox\"\n   155→        aria-activedescendant={activeDescendant}\n   156→        aria-label=\"Search for a book\"\n   157→        aria-autocomplete=\"list\"\n   158→      />\n   159→      {loading && (\n   160→        <div\n   161→          className=\"absolute right-3 top-3 text-xs\"\n   162→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   163→          role=\"status\"\n   164→          aria-live=\"polite\"\n   165→        >\n   166→          Searching...\n   167→        </div>\n   168→      )}\n   169→\n   170→      {open && results.length > 0 && (\n   171→        <div\n   172→          ref={listboxRef}\n   173→          id=\"book-search-listbox\"\n   174→          role=\"listbox\"\n   175→          aria-label=\"Book search results\"\n   176→          className=\"absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 card shadow-lg max-h-72 overflow-y-auto\"\n   177→          style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 0\" }}\n   178→        >\n   179→          {results.map((book, index) => (\n   180→            <div\n   181→              key={book.key}\n   182→              id={`book-option-${book.key}`}\n   183→              role=\"option\"\n   184→              aria-selected={index === activeIndex}\n   185→              onClick={() => handleSelect(book)}\n   186→              className=\"w-full text-left px-4 py-2 hover:bg-gray-50 flex items-center gap-3 cursor-pointer\"\n   187→              style={{\n   188→                backgroundColor: index === activeIndex ? \"rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04)\" : undefined,\n   189→              }}\n   190→            >\n   191→              {book.cover_i ? (\n   192→                <Image\n   193→                  src={`https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-S.jpg`}\n   194→                  alt={`Cover of ${book.title}`}\n   195→                  width={32}\n   196→                  height={48}\n   197→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 object-cover rounded\"\n   198→                />\n   199→              ) : (\n   200→                <div\n   201→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm\"\n   202→                  style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\" }}\n   203→                  aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   204→                >\n   205→                  <span role=\"img\" aria-hidden=\"true\">📖</span>\n   206→                </div>\n   207→              )}\n   208→              <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n   209→                <div\n   210→                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold truncate\"\n   211→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   212→                >\n   213→                  {book.title}\n   214→                </div>\n   215→                <div\n   216→                  className=\"text-xs truncate\"\n   217→                  style={{\n   218→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   219→                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   220→                  }}\n   221→                >\n   222→                  {book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\"}\n   223→                </div>\n   224→              </div>\n   225→            </div>\n   226→          ))}\n   227→        </div>\n   228→      )}\n   229→    </div>\n   230→  );\n   231→}\n   232→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"068828e4-a4f2-4fc3-8087-0d5f04047419","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:22.459Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017fDfVnPffL79wGi1SdRDwV","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n     6→ * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n     7→ * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n    11→ * - Author adds bot to their server\n    12→ * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n    13→ * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const CLIENT_ID = process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\n    18→const DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n    19→\n    20→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    21→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0 && CLIENT_ID.length > 0;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→/**\n    25→ * Validate a Discord snowflake ID (numeric string, 17-20 digits).\n    26→ * Used to prevent path traversal in Discord API URL construction.\n    27→ */\n    28→export function isValidSnowflake(id: string): boolean {\n    29→  return /^\\d{17,20}$/.test(id);\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→/**\n    33→ * Call the Discord REST API.\n    34→ */\n    35→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    36→  endpoint: string,\n    37→  method: string = \"GET\",\n    38→  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n    39→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n    40→  try {\n    41→    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n    42→      method,\n    43→      headers: {\n    44→        Authorization: `Bot ${BOT_TOKEN}`,\n    45→        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    46→      },\n    47→      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    48→    });\n    49→\n    50→    if (!res.ok) {\n    51→      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    52→      return {\n    53→        ok: false,\n    54→        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n    55→      };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    59→    return { ok: true, data };\n    60→  } catch (err) {\n    61→    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n    62→  }\n    63→}\n    64→\n    65→/**\n    66→ * Get bot user info.\n    67→ */\n    68→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    69→  id: string;\n    70→  username: string;\n    71→} | null> {\n    72→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n    73→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→/**\n    77→ * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n    78→ * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n    79→ *\n    80→ * Permission bits:\n    81→ * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n    82→ * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n    83→ * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n    84→ * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n    85→ * Total: 3089\n    86→ */\n    87→export function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n    88→  const permissions = 3089;\n    89→  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n    90→  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${CLIENT_ID}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n    91→}\n    92→\n    93→/**\n    94→ * Create a text channel in a guild.\n    95→ */\n    96→export async function createTextChannel(\n    97→  guildId: string,\n    98→  name: string,\n    99→  topic?: string\n   100→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   101→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   102→    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n   103→    \"POST\",\n   104→    {\n   105→      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n   106→      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n   107→      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n   108→    }\n   109→  );\n   110→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→/**\n   114→ * Create an invite for a channel.\n   115→ */\n   116→export async function createChannelInvite(\n   117→  channelId: string,\n   118→  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n   119→): Promise<string | null> {\n   120→  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n   121→    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n   122→    \"POST\",\n   123→    {\n   124→      max_age: maxAge,\n   125→      max_uses: 0,\n   126→      unique: true,\n   127→    }\n   128→  );\n   129→  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n   130→    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n   131→  }\n   132→  return null;\n   133→}\n   134→\n   135→/**\n   136→ * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n   137→ */\n   138→export async function sendChannelMessage(\n   139→  channelId: string,\n   140→  content: string\n   141→): Promise<boolean> {\n   142→  const res = await callApi(\n   143→    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n   144→    \"POST\",\n   145→    { content }\n   146→  );\n   147→  return res.ok;\n   148→}\n   149→\n   150→/**\n   151→ * Get guild (server) info.\n   152→ */\n   153→export async function getGuild(\n   154→  guildId: string\n   155→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   156→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   157→    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n   158→  );\n   159→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   160→}\n   161→\n   162→/**\n   163→ * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n   164→ */\n   165→export async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n   166→  { id: string; name: string }[]\n   167→> {\n   168→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n   169→    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n   170→  );\n   171→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n   172→}\n   173→\n   174→/**\n   175→ * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n   176→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   177→ */\n   178→export function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n   179→  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   180→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   181→}\n   182→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"f1dcc107-6030-41a2-b0c5-9d7a872e3fbc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:25.839Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JGjgWysbzZ4dQjvNxnbDad","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { isBotConfigured, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     3→import { safeCompare } from \"@/lib/crypto-utils\";\n     4→\n     5→/**\n     6→ * Discord bot setup endpoint.\n     7→ * Returns bot info and invite URL for verification.\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Protected by DISCORD_SETUP_SECRET env var.\n    10→ */\n    11→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const { secret } = await req.json();\n    13→  const setupSecret = process.env.DISCORD_SETUP_SECRET || \"\";\n    14→\n    15→  if (!setupSecret || !secret || typeof secret !== \"string\" || !safeCompare(secret, setupSecret)) {\n    16→    return NextResponse.json(\n    17→      { error: \"Invalid setup secret\" },\n    18→      { status: 403 }\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→\n    22→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json(\n    24→      {\n    25→        error:\n    26→          \"Discord bot is not configured. Set DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID environment variables.\",\n    27→      },\n    28→      { status: 503 }\n    29→    );\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const botInfo = await getBotInfo();\n    33→  if (!botInfo) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json(\n    35→      { error: \"Failed to connect to Discord API. Check your bot token.\" },\n    36→      { status: 500 }\n    37→    );\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  return NextResponse.json({\n    41→    ok: true,\n    42→    bot: {\n    43→      id: botInfo.id,\n    44→      username: botInfo.username,\n    45→    },\n    46→    message: `Discord bot \"${botInfo.username}\" is configured and ready.`,\n    47→  });\n    48→}\n    49→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"5d1659fe-33c2-448a-bda3-6b6122466a25","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:04:26.041Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUct8y6gPvU7fwAZQNypPT","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## Code Review: Bookmate Application\n\nReviewing all API routes under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/`, lib files under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/`, and page/component files under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/` and `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/`. Focus areas: memory leaks, performance, data integrity, edge cases, API consistency, error recovery, and session/cookie edge cases.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Orphaned Records on Listing Delete — `pending_telegram_groups` and `telegram_chats` Not Cleaned Up\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 308-314\n\nThe `DELETE` handler removes `listing_applications`, `listing_members`, `notifications`, `ratings`, and the listing itself. However, `pending_telegram_groups` is not deleted. If a listing is deleted while a Telegram group creation is in progress (i.e., a row exists in `pending_telegram_groups` for that `listing_id`), the row is left dangling. Because `foreign_keys = ON` is set, this will cause a foreign key constraint violation when the listing row is deleted, crashing the entire delete transaction with a 500 error and leaving the listing in a half-deleted state.\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nSQLite with `foreign_keys = ON` enforces FK constraints at the time of the DELETE. The `pending_telegram_groups` table has `FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)` with no `ON DELETE CASCADE`. The delete transaction in the route tries to delete from `listings` without first removing the `pending_telegram_groups` row, which will raise a constraint error.\n\n**Fix:**\n```typescript\ndb.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n```\nAdd this line to the `deleteTransaction` block in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, before line 313 (`DELETE FROM listings`).\n\n---\n\n### 2. `parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)` Called on Possibly-Non-String Input — Type Error at Runtime\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, line 230\n\nIn the PATCH handler, `maxGroupSize` is validated to be either `\"number\"` or `\"string\"` (line 180). However, on line 230, `parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)` is called. TypeScript's `parseInt` signature expects a `string` as its first argument. When `maxGroupSize` is already a number, passing it to `parseInt` works by accident (JavaScript coerces it to string first), but a floating-point number like `3.9` would pass the `typeof` check and `parseInt(\"3.9\", 10)` would silently produce `3`, bypassing the `isNaN` guard. The validated `size` value from line 183 is computed correctly (`parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10)`) but is never used — instead `newSize` is computed again at line 230 directly from the uncoerced value, and that `newSize` is what gets written to the database.\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\nThe safe parsed `size` from line 183 is used only for the member-count comparison check (line 193), but never stored. The stored value at line 232 is `newSize = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)`, computed redundantly outside the validation block. If `maxGroupSize` is `3.9` (a number), `parseInt(3.9, 10)` yields `3`, which passes the `size >= 2 && size <= 20` check but differs from the \"validated\" size. This is an inconsistency where validation and storage diverge.\n\n**Fix:** Replace line 230 with:\n```typescript\nconst newSize = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n```\nOr simply reuse the already-computed `size` variable from line 183 by restructuring the block so `size` is in scope.\n\n---\n\n## High Severity Issues\n\n### 3. `score` Field in Ratings API Not Type-Checked Before Arithmetic\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 51-56\n\n`score` is taken directly from the parsed JSON body without a `typeof` check before the arithmetic comparison `score < 1 || score > 5`. If `score` is a string like `\"3\"`, JavaScript coerces it during the comparison (`\"3\" < 1` is `false`, `\"3\" > 5` is `false`), so the validation passes. `Number.isInteger(\"3\")` returns `false`, which would catch this. However, a string like `\"3.0\"` would also pass `Number.isInteger` check... actually no, `Number.isInteger(\"3.0\")` returns `false`. But `\"3\"` passes neither the range check (`\"3\" > 5` is `false` via coercion) nor `Number.isInteger(\"3\")` (returns `false`). So string scores are blocked. The issue is specifically with a float like `3.5`: `typeof 3.5 !== \"number\"` is false (it IS a number), `3.5 < 1` is false, `3.5 > 5` is false, and `Number.isInteger(3.5)` is... false. So floats are correctly rejected. The validation is actually fine. I'm removing this as a false positive.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `comment` Field in Ratings API Has No Type Check\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 65-69\n\n`comment` is received from the request body and only checked for `comment.length > 1000`. If `comment` is not a string (e.g., a number or array), calling `.length` on it either returns the wrong value or throws. If `comment` is `[1, 2, 3]`, `comment.length` is `3` (passes), then `comment || \"\"` evaluates to the array, and `db.prepare(...).run(...)` would receive an array as the comment value. SQLite via better-sqlite3 would throw a binding error, producing a 500 response.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**Fix:**\n```typescript\nif (comment !== undefined && comment !== null && typeof comment !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid comment type\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\nif (comment && typeof comment === \"string\" && comment.length > 1000) { ... }\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. Open Library API Failure Produces Unhandled Network Error with Poor UX in `BookSearch`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`, lines 60-77\n\nWhen the Open Library API is down or times out, the `fetch` call throws a network error (not an `AbortError`). The catch block sets `setResults([])` but does NOT set any user-visible error state or `setLoading(false)`. The `finally` block only clears loading if `!controller.signal.aborted` — but on a generic network error the signal is not aborted, so loading IS cleared. However, there is no error message shown to the user. The search input just stays empty and appears to do nothing. Users have no indication that the book search service is unavailable.\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThis is squarely within the \"Error recovery — what happens when external APIs (Open Library) are down\" review scope.\n\n**Fix:** Add an error state to `BookSearch` and display a brief message (\"Book search is unavailable, please enter title manually\") when a non-abort error occurs. The parent `CreateListingPage` allows manual title entry but users have no affordance to know they should fall back to it.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Notification Count in Navbar Is Stale After Actions (Never Refreshed)\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/Navbar.tsx`, lines 22-34\n\nThe notification badge count (`notifCount`) is fetched once on mount via `/api/auth/me` and never updated. After a user reads notifications (navigates to `/notifications`, which marks all as read), returns to the home page, and performs an action that generates new notifications, the badge count remains stale for the entire session unless the page is fully refreshed. This is a UX correctness issue: the badge will show outdated counts or fail to show new notifications.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThere is no polling, no route-change re-fetch of the count, and no event-based mechanism to update `notifCount` after state changes. The count is initialized from `/api/auth/me` but never re-fetched. On navigation the `pathname` effect only closes menus; it does not re-fetch user data.\n\n**Fix:** Re-fetch `/api/auth/me` (or a lightweight `/api/notifications` count endpoint) when the pathname changes, or add a short polling interval (e.g., 60 seconds).\n\n---\n\n### 7. `pending_telegram_groups` Table Has No Index on `telegram_chat_id`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, lines 126-134\n\nThe `pending_telegram_groups` table is looked up in the Telegram webhook handler by `telegram_chat_id` (see `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, line 122). The primary key on this table is `listing_id`, not `telegram_chat_id`. The lookup `WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?` will perform a full table scan. While this table is likely small in practice, it is inconsistent with the explicit indexing strategy applied to all other FK columns in the schema (lines 170-177). More importantly, no foreign key or unique constraint exists on `telegram_chat_id`, so duplicate entries for the same chat ID are possible if a bot is added to the same group twice.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**Fix:** Add to the index block in `db.ts`:\n```sql\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pending_telegram_chat_id ON pending_telegram_groups(telegram_chat_id);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 8. Rate Limiter In-Memory Store Has Unbounded Growth Under High Load\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts`, lines 11-27\n\nThe cleanup interval runs every 5 minutes and removes entries with no recent timestamps. However, during a sustained attack or high-traffic period, the `store` Map grows without bound until the cleanup runs. Each unique `key` (IP + email combination for login attempts) adds a new entry. A simple IP rotation attack against the login endpoint — cycling through many source IPs — fills the store with O(attacker_IPs) entries each containing timestamps, consuming unbounded memory between cleanup cycles.\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nThe comment notes this is not shared across cluster workers, which is appropriate for the single-process SQLite setup. However, the missing bound on store size means a targeted attack can cause OOM. The `store` has no max-size cap.\n\n**Fix:** Add a max-size check before inserting new entries:\n```typescript\nif (store.size > 50_000 && !store.has(key)) {\n  // Under attack or very high traffic — reject new keys conservatively\n  return { allowed: false, retryAfter: 60 };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Medium Severity Issues\n\n### 9. Delete Listing Does Not Remove `pending_telegram_groups` or `telegram_user_links` Referencing the Listing\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 308-314\n\n(Separate from Issue 1 above.) `telegram_user_links` is not listing-scoped so this is not an orphan issue. However, `telegram_chats` entries associated with a deleted listing's `telegram_chat_id` are never cleaned up. This is a minor data leak — not a correctness bug since `telegram_chats` has no FK referencing `listings`. Confidence 65 — not reporting as high-confidence.\n\n---\n\n### 10. `notifyGroupFull` Silently Skips the Author Notification, But Author Is Always a Member\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 127-128\n\nThe query to fetch members for the `group_full` notification explicitly excludes the author (`user_id != ?`). The comment says \"Exclude the author — they already received a separate 'new_member' notification.\" However, the author receives a `new_member` notification when the *joiner* joins, not a `group_full` notification. This means the author never receives a notification that their own group is now full. This is a logic gap — the author is the person most interested in knowing the group is full (they need to create the Telegram/Discord group).\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\nLooking at the join flow: `notifyListingAuthor` sends a `new_member` notification to the author. `notifyGroupFull` is called afterward and explicitly excludes the author. No code path sends a `group_full` notification to the author.\n\n**Fix:** Either include the author in `notifyGroupFull`, or send a separate targeted notification in the join/approve routes after the group fills:\n```typescript\n// In notifyGroupFull, remove the author exclusion:\nconst members = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n  .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|\n| 1 | Critical | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:308-314` | FK violation crash: `pending_telegram_groups` not deleted before listing is deleted |\n| 2 | High | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:230` | `parseInt(maxGroupSize)` called directly; validated `size` not used for storage |\n| 4 | High | `app/api/ratings/route.ts:65-69` | `comment` field not type-checked before `.length` access |\n| 5 | High | `components/BookSearch.tsx:60-77` | Open Library API failure gives no user feedback; silent empty state |\n| 6 | High | `components/Navbar.tsx:22-34` | Notification count never re-fetched after initial mount; stale badge |\n| 7 | High | `lib/db.ts:128-134` | `pending_telegram_groups.telegram_chat_id` missing index; allows duplicate entries |\n| 8 | Medium | `lib/rate-limit.ts:11-27` | In-memory rate-limit store has no size cap; unbounded growth under attack |\n| 10 | Medium | `lib/notifications.ts:127-128` | Author never receives `group_full` notification for their own listing |"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: af032ea10c8e1c9f9 (use SendMessage with to: 'af032ea10c8e1c9f9' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 116043\ntool_uses: 71\nduration_ms: 238153</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"8da0f00d-0a9a-4bcb-83c3-2dec95d3ca22","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:06:39.631Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Perform a thorough code review of the Bookmate application in /root/Projects/bookmate/app.\n\nThis is a Next.js 15 App Router application with:\n- TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4\n- SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- iron-session for auth\n- API routes in app/api/\n\n9 previous rounds of code review have been done, covering:\n- Security (timing-safe secrets, XSS, input validation, SQL injection, webhook auth)\n- Race conditions (stale data in transactions, concurrent writes)\n- Client-side bugs (date handling, error boundaries, ARIA)\n- Auth bypass, HTML injection in emails, type validation\n\nFocus on areas NOT covered in previous rounds:\n1. **Memory leaks** — unclosed database connections, event listeners not cleaned up\n2. **Performance** — N+1 queries, missing DB indexes, unnecessary re-renders\n3. **Data integrity** — orphaned records when deleting, foreign key enforcement\n4. **Edge cases** — empty states, boundary conditions, Unicode handling\n5. **API consistency** — response format inconsistencies, HTTP status codes\n6. **Error recovery** — what happens when external APIs (Open Library) are down\n7. **Session/cookie edge cases** — expired sessions, cookie size limits\n\nRead through the key files in:\n- app/lib/ (db.ts, session.ts, notifications.ts, crypto-utils.ts)\n- app/app/api/ (all route handlers)\n- app/app/ (page components)\n- app/components/\n\nReport only HIGH-CONFIDENCE issues that are genuine bugs or significant problems. For each issue, provide:\n- Severity (Critical/High/Medium)\n- File and line number\n- What the bug/issue is\n- What the fix should be\n\nSkip cosmetic/style issues. Skip anything that was likely fixed in previous rounds.","agentId":"af032ea10c8e1c9f9","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## Code Review: Bookmate Application\n\nReviewing all API routes under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/`, lib files under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/`, and page/component files under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/` and `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/`. Focus areas: memory leaks, performance, data integrity, edge cases, API consistency, error recovery, and session/cookie edge cases.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Orphaned Records on Listing Delete — `pending_telegram_groups` and `telegram_chats` Not Cleaned Up\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 308-314\n\nThe `DELETE` handler removes `listing_applications`, `listing_members`, `notifications`, `ratings`, and the listing itself. However, `pending_telegram_groups` is not deleted. If a listing is deleted while a Telegram group creation is in progress (i.e., a row exists in `pending_telegram_groups` for that `listing_id`), the row is left dangling. Because `foreign_keys = ON` is set, this will cause a foreign key constraint violation when the listing row is deleted, crashing the entire delete transaction with a 500 error and leaving the listing in a half-deleted state.\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nSQLite with `foreign_keys = ON` enforces FK constraints at the time of the DELETE. The `pending_telegram_groups` table has `FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)` with no `ON DELETE CASCADE`. The delete transaction in the route tries to delete from `listings` without first removing the `pending_telegram_groups` row, which will raise a constraint error.\n\n**Fix:**\n```typescript\ndb.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n```\nAdd this line to the `deleteTransaction` block in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, before line 313 (`DELETE FROM listings`).\n\n---\n\n### 2. `parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)` Called on Possibly-Non-String Input — Type Error at Runtime\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, line 230\n\nIn the PATCH handler, `maxGroupSize` is validated to be either `\"number\"` or `\"string\"` (line 180). However, on line 230, `parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)` is called. TypeScript's `parseInt` signature expects a `string` as its first argument. When `maxGroupSize` is already a number, passing it to `parseInt` works by accident (JavaScript coerces it to string first), but a floating-point number like `3.9` would pass the `typeof` check and `parseInt(\"3.9\", 10)` would silently produce `3`, bypassing the `isNaN` guard. The validated `size` value from line 183 is computed correctly (`parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10)`) but is never used — instead `newSize` is computed again at line 230 directly from the uncoerced value, and that `newSize` is what gets written to the database.\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\nThe safe parsed `size` from line 183 is used only for the member-count comparison check (line 193), but never stored. The stored value at line 232 is `newSize = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)`, computed redundantly outside the validation block. If `maxGroupSize` is `3.9` (a number), `parseInt(3.9, 10)` yields `3`, which passes the `size >= 2 && size <= 20` check but differs from the \"validated\" size. This is an inconsistency where validation and storage diverge.\n\n**Fix:** Replace line 230 with:\n```typescript\nconst newSize = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n```\nOr simply reuse the already-computed `size` variable from line 183 by restructuring the block so `size` is in scope.\n\n---\n\n## High Severity Issues\n\n### 3. `score` Field in Ratings API Not Type-Checked Before Arithmetic\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 51-56\n\n`score` is taken directly from the parsed JSON body without a `typeof` check before the arithmetic comparison `score < 1 || score > 5`. If `score` is a string like `\"3\"`, JavaScript coerces it during the comparison (`\"3\" < 1` is `false`, `\"3\" > 5` is `false`), so the validation passes. `Number.isInteger(\"3\")` returns `false`, which would catch this. However, a string like `\"3.0\"` would also pass `Number.isInteger` check... actually no, `Number.isInteger(\"3.0\")` returns `false`. But `\"3\"` passes neither the range check (`\"3\" > 5` is `false` via coercion) nor `Number.isInteger(\"3\")` (returns `false`). So string scores are blocked. The issue is specifically with a float like `3.5`: `typeof 3.5 !== \"number\"` is false (it IS a number), `3.5 < 1` is false, `3.5 > 5` is false, and `Number.isInteger(3.5)` is... false. So floats are correctly rejected. The validation is actually fine. I'm removing this as a false positive.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `comment` Field in Ratings API Has No Type Check\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 65-69\n\n`comment` is received from the request body and only checked for `comment.length > 1000`. If `comment` is not a string (e.g., a number or array), calling `.length` on it either returns the wrong value or throws. If `comment` is `[1, 2, 3]`, `comment.length` is `3` (passes), then `comment || \"\"` evaluates to the array, and `db.prepare(...).run(...)` would receive an array as the comment value. SQLite via better-sqlite3 would throw a binding error, producing a 500 response.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**Fix:**\n```typescript\nif (comment !== undefined && comment !== null && typeof comment !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid comment type\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\nif (comment && typeof comment === \"string\" && comment.length > 1000) { ... }\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. Open Library API Failure Produces Unhandled Network Error with Poor UX in `BookSearch`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`, lines 60-77\n\nWhen the Open Library API is down or times out, the `fetch` call throws a network error (not an `AbortError`). The catch block sets `setResults([])` but does NOT set any user-visible error state or `setLoading(false)`. The `finally` block only clears loading if `!controller.signal.aborted` — but on a generic network error the signal is not aborted, so loading IS cleared. However, there is no error message shown to the user. The search input just stays empty and appears to do nothing. Users have no indication that the book search service is unavailable.\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThis is squarely within the \"Error recovery — what happens when external APIs (Open Library) are down\" review scope.\n\n**Fix:** Add an error state to `BookSearch` and display a brief message (\"Book search is unavailable, please enter title manually\") when a non-abort error occurs. The parent `CreateListingPage` allows manual title entry but users have no affordance to know they should fall back to it.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Notification Count in Navbar Is Stale After Actions (Never Refreshed)\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/Navbar.tsx`, lines 22-34\n\nThe notification badge count (`notifCount`) is fetched once on mount via `/api/auth/me` and never updated. After a user reads notifications (navigates to `/notifications`, which marks all as read), returns to the home page, and performs an action that generates new notifications, the badge count remains stale for the entire session unless the page is fully refreshed. This is a UX correctness issue: the badge will show outdated counts or fail to show new notifications.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThere is no polling, no route-change re-fetch of the count, and no event-based mechanism to update `notifCount` after state changes. The count is initialized from `/api/auth/me` but never re-fetched. On navigation the `pathname` effect only closes menus; it does not re-fetch user data.\n\n**Fix:** Re-fetch `/api/auth/me` (or a lightweight `/api/notifications` count endpoint) when the pathname changes, or add a short polling interval (e.g., 60 seconds).\n\n---\n\n### 7. `pending_telegram_groups` Table Has No Index on `telegram_chat_id`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, lines 126-134\n\nThe `pending_telegram_groups` table is looked up in the Telegram webhook handler by `telegram_chat_id` (see `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, line 122). The primary key on this table is `listing_id`, not `telegram_chat_id`. The lookup `WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?` will perform a full table scan. While this table is likely small in practice, it is inconsistent with the explicit indexing strategy applied to all other FK columns in the schema (lines 170-177). More importantly, no foreign key or unique constraint exists on `telegram_chat_id`, so duplicate entries for the same chat ID are possible if a bot is added to the same group twice.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**Fix:** Add to the index block in `db.ts`:\n```sql\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pending_telegram_chat_id ON pending_telegram_groups(telegram_chat_id);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 8. Rate Limiter In-Memory Store Has Unbounded Growth Under High Load\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts`, lines 11-27\n\nThe cleanup interval runs every 5 minutes and removes entries with no recent timestamps. However, during a sustained attack or high-traffic period, the `store` Map grows without bound until the cleanup runs. Each unique `key` (IP + email combination for login attempts) adds a new entry. A simple IP rotation attack against the login endpoint — cycling through many source IPs — fills the store with O(attacker_IPs) entries each containing timestamps, consuming unbounded memory between cleanup cycles.\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nThe comment notes this is not shared across cluster workers, which is appropriate for the single-process SQLite setup. However, the missing bound on store size means a targeted attack can cause OOM. The `store` has no max-size cap.\n\n**Fix:** Add a max-size check before inserting new entries:\n```typescript\nif (store.size > 50_000 && !store.has(key)) {\n  // Under attack or very high traffic — reject new keys conservatively\n  return { allowed: false, retryAfter: 60 };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Medium Severity Issues\n\n### 9. Delete Listing Does Not Remove `pending_telegram_groups` or `telegram_user_links` Referencing the Listing\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 308-314\n\n(Separate from Issue 1 above.) `telegram_user_links` is not listing-scoped so this is not an orphan issue. However, `telegram_chats` entries associated with a deleted listing's `telegram_chat_id` are never cleaned up. This is a minor data leak — not a correctness bug since `telegram_chats` has no FK referencing `listings`. Confidence 65 — not reporting as high-confidence.\n\n---\n\n### 10. `notifyGroupFull` Silently Skips the Author Notification, But Author Is Always a Member\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 127-128\n\nThe query to fetch members for the `group_full` notification explicitly excludes the author (`user_id != ?`). The comment says \"Exclude the author — they already received a separate 'new_member' notification.\" However, the author receives a `new_member` notification when the *joiner* joins, not a `group_full` notification. This means the author never receives a notification that their own group is now full. This is a logic gap — the author is the person most interested in knowing the group is full (they need to create the Telegram/Discord group).\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\nLooking at the join flow: `notifyListingAuthor` sends a `new_member` notification to the author. `notifyGroupFull` is called afterward and explicitly excludes the author. No code path sends a `group_full` notification to the author.\n\n**Fix:** Either include the author in `notifyGroupFull`, or send a separate targeted notification in the join/approve routes after the group fills:\n```typescript\n// In notifyGroupFull, remove the author exclusion:\nconst members = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n  .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|\n| 1 | Critical | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:308-314` | FK violation crash: `pending_telegram_groups` not deleted before listing is deleted |\n| 2 | High | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:230` | `parseInt(maxGroupSize)` called directly; validated `size` not used for storage |\n| 4 | High | `app/api/ratings/route.ts:65-69` | `comment` field not type-checked before `.length` access |\n| 5 | High | `components/BookSearch.tsx:60-77` | Open Library API failure gives no user feedback; silent empty state |\n| 6 | High | `components/Navbar.tsx:22-34` | Notification count never re-fetched after initial mount; stale badge |\n| 7 | High | `lib/db.ts:128-134` | `pending_telegram_groups.telegram_chat_id` missing index; allows duplicate entries |\n| 8 | Medium | `lib/rate-limit.ts:11-27` | In-memory rate-limit store has no size cap; unbounded growth under attack |\n| 10 | Medium | `lib/notifications.ts:127-128` | Author never receives `group_full` notification for their own listing |"}],"totalDurationMs":238153,"totalTokens":116043,"totalToolUseCount":71,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":229,"cache_read_input_tokens":112234,"output_tokens":3579,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":229},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

AND user_id != ?\")\n   305→      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   306→\n   307→    // Delete in a transaction (notifications sent after, so they aren't wiped)\n   308→    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   309→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   310→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   311→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   312→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   313→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   314→    });\n   315→\n   316→    deleteTransaction();\n   317→\n   318→    // Send deletion notifications after transaction (listing_id is NULL since listing is gone)\n   319→    for (const member of members) {\n   320→      try {\n   321→        createNotification(\n   322→          member.user_id,\n   323→          null,\n   324→          \"listing_deleted\","}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3e46bfc1-1452-4963-9bd1-e4bd3fcd1a8e","uuid":"00ff8d5b-a84c-4d29-9cc9-a4a9bfd9d098","timestamp":"2026-03-24T07:06:45.754Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts","content":"    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  try {\n    // Get members to notify (excluding the author)\n    const members = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n    // Delete in a transaction (notifications sent after, so they aren't wiped)\n    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    });\n\n    deleteTransaction();\n\n    // Send deletion notifications after transaction (listing_id is NULL since listing is gone)\n    for (const member of members) {\n      try {\n        createNotification(\n          member.user_id,\n          null,\n          \"listing_deleted\",","numLines":30,"startLine":295,"totalLines":338}}}
